div1.entryFree:
Fulness, 1) completeness: John II, 440. H6B I, 1, 35.
2) plenty, affluence: with ample and brim f. of his force, H5 I, 2, 150. my plenteous joys, wanton in f. Mcb. I, 4, 34. to lapse in f. is sorer than to lie for need, Cymb. III, 6, 12.
3) satiety: although to-day thou fill thy hungry eyes even till they wink with f. Sonn. 56, 6.
